summary: The Arab world's poorest country, engulfed in the world's worst humanitarian crisis since 2015, when a Saudi-led coalition intervened against Houthi rebels backed by Iran — making it the centrepiece of the Saudi-Iran proxy war.

history.detail: Ottoman defeat; Zaydi Imamate rules north.
Britain leaves Aden; Marxist state established in south.
North and South Yemen unite; brief civil war 1994.
Zaydi Shia Houthi movement rises against Sanaa government.
Protests force long-time president Saleh from power.
Houthis seize Sanaa; Saudi coalition bombs Yemen; conflict becomes proxy war.
In solidarity with Gaza; Houthi missiles and drones disrupt 15% of global trade through Red Sea.

finance.currency_role: Yemeni Rial (YER) — collapsed; two separate currencies in north/south

resources.oil_gas: Declining oil; war has destroyed production
resources.water: Among world's most water-scarce; Sana'a may be first capital to run dry
resources.agriculture: Qat (mild stimulant) uses 40% of water; subsistence farming

follow_the_money.detail: Saudi Arabia spent $100B+ on Yemen war using US and UK weapons
Shipping companies pay diversion costs; some vessels targeted regardless

theories_disputed.topic: Western arms to Saudi Arabia for Yemen
theories_disputed.detail: US and UK sold billions in arms to Saudi Arabia used in Yemen despite documented civilian strikes. UK courts confirmed exports were unlawful.

connections.detail: Saudi-led coalition vs Houthi rebels since 2015
Iran arms and advises Houthis; drones, missiles, intelligence
